channel.title: Jonas B. Skånøy
channel.description: Recent content on Jonas B. Skånøy

item.title: My thoughts on this semester.
item.description: This semester is going to be my final one as a bachelor student in philosophy! Am very excited to get started, and whilst what comes after is uncertain, I am very grateful for the chance I’ve been given to actually just study something for the sake of liking the subject. I am grateful for having a stable and safe family dynamic, who I can rely on both emotionally, but also with regards to practical manners.

item.title: Problems Regarding Political Debates in this Day and Age.
item.description: In this blog post, I want to put out thoughts on the topic of truth. More specifically, I want to look at how the modern understanding of truth as a rhetorical tool, rather than something that is practical in our everyday life. My motivation for writing this to get my thought out on paper, as well as to express my disagreements with Ben Shapiro and Jordan Peterson on this specific topic.

item.title: Book review: Twilight of the Idols
item.description: In this post, I’ll review Nietzsche’s famous work Götzen-Dämmerung, better known as “Twilight of the Idols”. I will try, to the best of my abilities, bringing something forth the chapters I found most fruitful and thought-provoking. So, without further ado, let’s begin! Quick disclosure This work might be one Nietzsche’s least nuanced work, and a lot of the stands he makes in it is a result of his declining health.

item.title: Welcome to the blog
item.description: So, it’s about time for me to start blogging! I’ve been putting off the idea for quite some time now (Just wanted everything else on the site to be in order first), but finally I am doing it. I’ll for the most part make blog posts on things that interested me - that being continental philosophy -, and technologies that takes my attention. I will also be posting updates to the sites here, when that’s necessary.

About me Hi! 👋 Welcome to my website, powered by Hugo, and written in GO. Massive shoutout to Luiz F. A. de Prá for making this amazing theme, called Coder. So, as you might tell, my name is Jonas Skånøy. I reside in Norway, and currently I’m stuyding for a bachelors degree in philosophy at NTNU. Before this however, I worked as a apprentice at a IT-consultant company, called HEMIT.

☎️ Digital Contact Information I’m not on Telegram, Twitter or Reddit. So email and github will have to do. 📧 Email: jonas.skanoy@e.email Github: jonasskanoy 📕 Rules Don’t contact me about gossip or political opinions, should go without saying these days. Don’t contact me asking me to shill your product unless it is all free software. I am not really a hardcore tech consumer.

Donation info Donations are by all means optional – they are not necessary for my well-being. However, if you feel like donating anyway, I’ve made some donations options – both for the normies and the tech-savvy. Accepted payment methods How to donate QR-codes Paypal Vipps Donations through Vipps can be sent in through the QR-code on the right, or by clicking here! Monero 44ahayqrGsS2m9fcs3eKjmePd7kdeePAC3YdCR4xqfLihLWJLHA2gZW4owwhdjUQKyNgwr7VQNFJLcX8v28XuFeJHfsGtXy

Personal library This is a sorted list of the books I currently own. It’s categorized according to it’s genre, i.e. novels and philosophy. Keep in mind that most of these are made from norwegian publishers, so there might not be a english copy of some of the works printed here. The works I have read will be marked with a “*”. Philosophy, Psychology, Logic and Aesthetics Aristotle. (2010). On the Soul.
